LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT MITCHELL ENGINEERING/OBAYASHI CORPORATION, LUMBERMENS MUTUAL CASUALTY COMPANY, NATIONAL FIRE INSURANCE COMPANY OF HARTFORD, UNITED STATES FIDELITY AND GUARANTY COMPANY, ST.PAUL FIRE AND MARINE INSURANCE COMPANY, AMERICAN HOME ASSURANCE COMPANY, NATIONAL UNION FIRE INSURANCE OF PITTSBURG, AND ARCH INSURANCE COMPANY'S, MOTION TO STRIKE THE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT IN INTERVENTION. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: MOTION TO STRIKE IS DENIED. NO SHOWING THAT THE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT DOES NOT COMPLY WITH CCP SECTION 387. COURT ORDER OF APRIL 28, 2008 MAKES NO SUBSTANTIVE RULINGS OR CONSTRUCTIONS ON FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT, BUT MERELY GRANTS LEAVE TO INTERVENE. SUFFICIENT FACTS PLED TO SUPPORT A CONTROVERSY BETWEEN THE RIGHTS OF PACIFIC SAND & GRAVEL AND OF BAY BUSINESS CREDIT. JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR #5797

Off Calendar Apr-08-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT MITCHELL ENGINEERING/OBAYASHI CORPORATION, LUMBERMENS MUTUAL CASUALTY COMPANY, NATIONAL FIRE INSURANCE COMPANY OF HARTFORD, UNITED STATES FIDELITY AND GUARANTY COMPANY, ST.PAUL FIRE AND MARINE INSURANCE COMPANY, AMERICAN HOME ASSURANCE COMPANY, NATIONAL UNION FIRE INSURANCE OF PITTSBURG, and ARCH INSURANCE COMPANY'S, Motion To Strike The First Amended Complaint In Intervention. Appearances: Ginger L. Sotelo, Esq. of Pahl & McCay (408) 286-5100, appearing for Assignee of pacific Sand & Fravel, Inc, Bay Business Credit, and opposing party; Cassiana Aaronson, Esq. of McInerney & Dillon (510) 465-7100, appearing for Defendants and moving party. Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Motion to strike is denied. No showing that the first amended complaint does not comply with CCP section 387. Court order of April 28, 2008 makes no substantive rulings or constructions on first amended complaint, but merely grants leave to intervene. Sufficient facts pled to support a controversy between the rights of Pacific Sand & Gravel and of Bay Business Credit. (302CWW/JW)Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard, Reporter: Kent Gubbine, CSR 5759
